meta.name: Caribbean Fried Plantains
meta.subtitle: Sweet maduros or savory tostones-style slices (pan-fried)

source.author: SSA Canonical Catalog
source.attribution: Modeled from common Caribbean home-cooking plantain preparations (ripe sweet slices and green twice-fried-style).

yield.notes: One medium plantain serves 1–2 depending on what else is served.

ingredients.label: Plantains
ingredients.notes: Use yellow/black-speckled for sweet maduros; use green for savory twice-fry style.

ingredients.label: Neutral frying oil
ingredients.notes: Enough for shallow frying (about 1/4–1/2 inch depth).

ingredients.label: Salt
ingredients.notes: Season to taste. For sweet plantains, a tiny pinch makes them pop.

ingredients.label: Brown sugar
ingredients.notes: Optional for very ripe plantains if you want extra caramel notes.

steps.title: Peel and slice
instructions: Cut off both ends of each plantain.
Score the peel lengthwise (1–3 lines), then peel away the skin.
Slice on a diagonal into 1/2-inch pieces (sweet version) OR into 1-inch chunks (green plantain option).
Pat slices dry if they seem wet—less splatter and better browning.

steps.title: Heat oil for shallow frying
instructions: Add oil to a skillet to reach about 1/4–1/2 inch depth.
Heat over medium to medium-high until shimmering.
If using a thermometer, aim for ~350°F for ripe plantains; ~360°F for green plantains.

steps.title: Fry until golden
instructions: Carefully add plantain slices in a single layer (work in batches).
Fry 2–3 minutes per side for ripe plantains until deep golden with caramelized edges.
For green plantains (savory): fry 2 minutes per side until pale golden and slightly tender.
Transfer to paper towels or a rack to drain.

steps.title: Season and serve
instructions: Season immediately with a pinch of salt.
Optional sweet finish: dust with cinnamon and/or a tiny sprinkle of brown sugar.
Optional bright finish: a few drops of lime juice.
Serve hot as a side or snack.

adapt.notes: Use green plantains. After first fry, smash each piece (using a cup or tostonera), then fry again 1–2 minutes per side until crisp. Salt more generously; serve with garlic sauce or lime.
Toss slices lightly with oil. Air fry at 380°F for ~10–14 minutes, flipping halfway, until browned.
Use just-ripe yellow plantains (not black), skip sugar, finish only with salt + lime.

reheat.notes: Re-crisp at 375°F for 5–8 minutes. Avoid microwave if you want crisp edges.

makeAhead: Slice plantains up to 30 minutes ahead; keep covered to prevent drying
